tasks.title: Instant-tier CI gates (lint, tidy, advisory audit) + dev trigger
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change harden-ci-correctness-gates, tasks.md ┬¦1, design Decisions 2/3/4/5. CI today is a single `go test -count=1 ./...` job in .github/workflows/test.yml (triggers: push to main + pull_request). These gates are all things the tree already passes or that have near-zero false positives.

Outcome: CI gains a `lint` job (vet + gofmt), a `tidy` job (mod-tidy drift), and an advisory `audit` job (govulncheck), and the whole gate suite also triggers on pushes to `dev`.

Files & scope: .github/workflows/test.yml.
- Add `dev` to `push.branches` (keep `main`; leave `pull_request` as-is).
- `lint` job: checkout + setup-go (`go-version-file: go.mod`); `go vet ./...`; then assert `gofmt -l .` is empty and fail listing the offending files if not. No laps/opencode/real-agent setup (it runs no tests). Mirrors `just check`.
- `tidy` job: checkout + setup-go; `go mod tidy`; then `git diff --exit-code go.mod go.sum` (fail with the diff).
- `audit` job: checkout + setup-go; `go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest`; `govulncheck ./...` with `continue-on-error: true` (ADVISORY ŌĆö design Decision 2). Do NOT make it a required check.

Design: the `dev` push-trigger is the prerequisite for branch protection ŌĆö a `main` that the release flow fast-forwards to a `dev` SHA must already carry that SHA's green checks (Decision 5). The instant tier finds nothing on today's tree, so any finding is a real latent issue.

Acceptance: tasks.md ┬¦1.1-1.5. On a pushed branch / PR, `lint` and `tidy` pass and `audit` runs advisory (non-blocking). No Go code changes expected.

tasks.title: Race detector CI job (separate, non-real-agent)
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md ┬¦2, design Decision 1. The relay E2E suite drives `Run()` concurrently with store reads ŌĆö exactly where the 0.11.5 `Store` data race lived ŌĆö but CI never runs `-race` today.

Outcome: a separate `race` job runs `go test -race -shuffle=on -count=1 ./...` over the non-real-agent suite.

Files & scope: .github/workflows/test.yml. Add a `race` job that MIRRORS the existing `test` job's tool installs (laps via `go install ŌĆ”/laps@<the same pinned version the test job uses>`, opencode install) but OMITS the `RALLY_TEST_REAL_AGENTS` env and the opencode-auth step, then runs `go test -race -shuffle=on -count=1 ./...`.

Design: a separate parallel job keeps the fast plain-`test` job as the quick signal and isolates the 2-20x race slowdown. Dropping real-agents trims the slowest/flakiest tests; races hide in Rally's own orchestration (store/runner/relay), which the non-real-agent suite already exercises, not in the external CLIs. `ubuntu-latest` has CGO for `-race`.

Acceptance: tasks.md ┬¦2.1-2.2 ŌĆö the `race` job passes on the current tree (race-clean as of 0.11.5). If `-race` surfaces a real race, that is a genuine latent bug: add a focused fix lap at the head of the queue (`laps add head ŌĆ”`) rather than weakening the gate. (Task 2.3 ŌĆö trimming the laps/opencode installs if the non-real-agent subset proves not to need them ŌĆö is optional and post-observation.)

tasks.title: Mirror every gate in `just` (local == CI)
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md ┬¦3, design Decision 8. The `local == CI` contract: every CI gate gets a one-word local equivalent so contributors and the release flow reproduce CI before pushing.

Outcome: `justfile` gains `test-race`, `audit`, and `tidy-check` recipes; the existing `check` (vet + gofmt) recipe is unchanged.

Files & scope: justfile.
- `test-race`: `go test -race -shuffle=on -count=1 ./...`.
- `audit`: `govulncheck ./...` (document the one-time `go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest`).
- `tidy-check`: `go mod tidy` then `git diff --exit-code go.mod go.sum`.
- Leave `check` exactly as it is.

Acceptance: tasks.md ┬¦3.1-3.4 ŌĆö all four recipes (`check`, `test-race`, `audit`, `tidy-check`) run green locally.

tasks.title: Release-flow wiring + branch-protection docs/enablement
tasks.description:
Context: tasks.md ┬¦4 and ┬¦5, design Decisions 5/6/7. The judgment piece: make branch protection on `main` compatible with the direct-push (devŌåÆmain fast-forward) release flow, and wire the gates into rally-release.

Outcome: the `rally-release` skill runs the new gates locally and verifies `dev` CI is green before the fast-forward; branch protection on `main` is documented and ready to enable; the hardened end state is pointed at the sibling change.

Files & scope:
- .claude/skills/rally-release/ : add the four `just` gates (`check`, `test-race`, `tidy-check`, `audit`) to the local-checks step (4.1); add a pre-fast-forward step that verifies the `dev` commit's required checks are green (e.g. `gh run list` / `gh pr checks` on the `dev` SHA) and STOPS, surfacing the failing checks, if not ŌĆö before the `dev`ŌåÆ`main` fast-forward (4.2, Decision 7). Keep this consistent with the skill's existing stop-on-failure contract.
- Repo docs (README "Releasing"/CI section or AGENTS.md): document the required checks (test, race, lint, tidy), "Include administrators" OFF on first rollout (Decision 6), and the advisory `audit`/govulncheck job being non-blocking. State explicitly that the hardened end state ŌĆö flipping govulncheck to required and "Include administrators" on ŌĆö is owned by the sibling `adopt-lint-and-fuzz-gates` change (its section G), not this one (5.3).
- Provide a ready-to-run `gh api` command / checklist to enable the branch protection on `main` (required checks = test, race, lint, tidy; NOT audit; admins excluded) (5.1).

Design: enabling branch protection (5.1) and smoke-testing it (5.2) are OPERATOR steps that happen post-merge, once the required jobs have green history on `dev` and `main` ŌĆö a feature branch has no such history yet. Deliver everything implementable (release-flow wiring, docs, the enablement command) and clearly flag actual enablement as the post-merge step. Do NOT attempt to enable branch protection from this branch.

Acceptance: tasks.md ┬¦4.1-4.2 and ┬¦5.3 implemented; ┬¦5.1-5.2 captured as documented/scripted post-merge operator steps with a ready `gh api` command. The rally-release changes are coherent and do not break its existing flow.

tasks.title: Final full-change verification (harden-ci-correctness-gates)
tasks.description:
Final OpenSpec verification. Use the openspec-verify-change skill against harden-ci-correctness-gates and inspect the whole diff.

This work is on branch `harden-ci-correctness-gates` (a worktree). Identify the intended merge target ŌĆö most likely `dev`, but confirm from the user / branch config / recent history, do NOT assume `main` ŌĆö then `git diff <target>...HEAD`. Identify the first lap/try of this batch and treat earlier branch work as pre-existing.

Confirm the end state (trace it, do not just rely on CI being green):
- Five CI jobs exist (test, race, lint, tidy, audit); `audit`/govulncheck is advisory (`continue-on-error`) and NOT in any required-check set.
- The gate suite triggers on `dev` and `main` pushes and on PRs (the branch-protection prerequisite).
- Each gate has a reproducing `just` recipe (`test`, `check`, `test-race`, `audit`, `tidy-check`) ŌĆö local == CI.
- No binary-behaviour change: `internal/buildinfo/VERSION` is untouched and the diff is confined to .github/workflows/, justfile, .claude/skills/rally-release/, and docs.
- `rally-release` runs the gates locally and verifies `dev` is green before the fast-forward.
- Branch protection is documented with administrators excluded on first rollout, and the govulncheckŌåÆblocking / adminsŌåÆon flip is attributed to the sibling `adopt-lint-and-fuzz-gates` change. (Tasks ┬¦5.1/┬¦5.2 are post-merge operator steps ŌĆö verify they are correctly documented/scripted, not that protection is live.)

Check off the tasks.md boxes for work verified correct and thorough. For substantive gaps, add focused head laps rather than fixing large issues inline. Do not rewrite git history; prefer additive/revert commits.
